description: Live capture from 22 05 48 - Seismic Controls (false exemption).docx

finding.issue: The section states that seismic restraint of piping systems is not required for the project. This is a false blanket exemption. Under DSA jurisdiction, CBC 2025 (Chapter 16) adopts ASCE 7-22 Chapter 13, which requires seismic bracing and anchorage of plumbing/mechanical piping unless the specific, conditional exemptions of ASCE 7-22 §13.6.8 are met on an item-by-item basis. A project-wide exemption will be rejected by the DSA plan checker and is a code-compliance/safety showstopper.
finding.existingText: A. Seismic restraint of piping systems is not required for this project.
finding.replacementText: A. Seismic restraint and bracing of piping systems shall be provided in accordance with CBC 2025 and ASCE 7-22 Chapter 13. Exemptions shall be applied only on an individual-component basis where explicitly permitted by ASCE 7-22 Section 13.6.8 (e.g., qualifying trapeze-supported or individually-supported piping within the prescribed weight, diameter, and hanger-length limits). Bracing shall use DSA-recognized OSHPD/OPM (OPM/OPA) seismic preapprovals or a project-specific design prepared and stamped by a California-licensed engineer.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: The finding's substance is valid — ASCE 7-22 Chapter 13 (adopted via CBC 2025) requires seismic support of piping with only conditional exemptions, so a project-wide exemption is improper — but the citation is wrong for the ASCE 7-22 edition: piping and tubing systems are in §13.6.7 (exemptions in §13.6.7.3), not §13.6.8 (which was the older ASCE 7-16 number). DSA IR 16-13 confirms the item-by-item trapeze/individual-support limits.
captured_verifier_response.correction: A. Seismic restraint and bracing of piping systems shall be provided in accordance with CBC 2025 and ASCE 7-22 Chapter 13. Exemptions shall be applied only on an individual-component basis where explicitly permitted by ASCE 7-22 Section 13.6.7 (Distribution Systems: Piping and Tubing Systems), including the conditional exemptions of Section 13.6.7.3 (e.g., qualifying trapeze-supported or individually-supported piping within the prescribed weight, diameter, and hanger-length limits). Bracing shall use DSA-recognized OSHPD/OPM (OPM/OPA) seismic preapprovals or a project-specific design prepared and stamped by a California-licensed engineer.

ground_truth.notes: Auto-captured for labeled defect: Declares seismic restraint of piping not required — wrong for a California DSA K-12 project. Confirm correct_verdict / expected_status before trusting this fixture.
